title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Box Office Opening
text: Sony's horror sequel underperformed expectations during the MLK weekend. The film earned an estimated $15 million over four days ($13 million for the three-day weekend), falling significantly short of tracking predictions. The opening was substantially weaker than its predecessor. 28 Years Later had debuted with $30 million during its three-day opening in June 2025 before reaching $151 million globally. Industry analysts had projected The Bone Temple would launch to approximately $20 million domestically. Despite the soft box office start, the film received strong reviews. It holds a 94% rating on Rotten Tomatoes and earned an A- CinemaScore, described as rare for horror films. The picture also received a 4.5 PostTrak rating. Sony executives remain optimistic about the film's potential staying power through word-of-mouth. The original 28 Days Later (2002) had opened to just $10 million before becoming a cultural phenomenon that redefined zombie cinema, suggesting the sequel could follow a similar trajectory with audience enthusiasm. The film stars Ralph Fiennes, Jack O'Connell, and Alfie Williams, with direction by Nia DaCosta.
